Module com.example.escriturarapida
Package com.example.escriturarapida.utilities

Class GameData

java.lang.Object
com.example.escriturarapida.utilities.GameData
public class GameData extends Object
Utility class that holds shared game state across scenes. All fields are static, meaning there is only one copy in memory accessible from any class without needing to create an instance.

This class acts as a simple data transfer object between controllers, persisting values when navigating from the game screen to the results screen.

Values for reasonWinOrLose:

  • 0 â€” Default, game has not ended yet.
  • 1 â€” Player won by completing all 45 levels.
  • 2 â€” Player won on the last level just as time ran out.
  • 3 â€” Player lost because time ran out.

    The reason the game ended, used by PauseController to display the appropriate win or lose message.

  • Field Details

    • level

      public static int level
      The level reached by the player when the game ended. Reset to 0 when the player restarts or exits to the start screen.

    • seconds

      public static int seconds
      The remaining seconds on the timer when the game ended. Reset to 0 when the player restarts or exits to the start screen.

    • milliseconds

      public static int milliseconds
      The remaining milliseconds on the timer when the game ended. Reset to 0 when the player restarts or exits to the start screen.

    • reasonWinOrLose

      public static int reasonWinOrLose
      The reason the game ended, used by PauseController to display the appropriate win or lose message. Reset to 0 when the player restarts or exits to the start screen.
